arts.ttl: Arsenal handed blow as Real Madrid make announcement on eve of Champions League clash
arts.sum: Arsenal host Real Madrid at the Emirates on Tuesday for the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final tie and the visitors have received a major boost on the injury front

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta makes SHOCK decision in striker chase
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly set to target a summer move for Blackburn Rovers forward Harvey Higgins as their pursuit of a new striker takes a surprise turn. Although Arsenal are eager to bring Higgins to the Emirates Stadium this summer, he will almost certainly join up with the club’s youth teams, rather than play in the senior side, if the Gunners are able to finalise a deal.

arts.ttl: ‘The arrogance is astounding’: How Real Madrid became the envy of world football
arts.sum: Arsenal will feel the weight and ‘aura’ of Real Madrid when they meet in the Champions League quarter-finals

arts.ttl: Agent said to confirm Chelsea and Real Madrid’s interest, won’t rush decision – Blues ‘swoop in’ for youngster
arts.sum: Albacete Balompié’s Christian Kofane has ended up on Chelsea and Real Madrid’s transfer agenda. That’s according to Africa Foot, and their title says Chelsea and Real Madrid are ‘swooping in’ for the 18-year-old. The striker joined the Segunda División side in January and managed four goals and an assist from 12 games. arts.sum: Real Madrid will look to continue their push for a 16th Champions League title at the Emirates

arts.ttl: Arsenal must forget Everton refereeing disaster as Arteta targets Real Madrid
arts.sum: football.london asked Arteta after the match as to his thoughts on the game and of course the penalty. Stop, start, no control of the match and then a decision to award a penalty based on very little which subsequently VAR determined it not enough to be considered a clear and obvious error.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid confirm huge fitness boost ahead of Arsenal quarter-final clash
arts.sum: Real Madrid's injury woes were compounded at the weekend with both Thibaut Courtois and Andriy Lunin forced out of La Liga action.
